figures.caption: Figure 1: DiBA provides a structured approximation to a dense matrix using three real diagonal factors and two 0/1 binary factors; k controls the storage–accuracy trade-off.
Table 1: Candidate and selected matrices in Experiment 1. Categories are functional labels; the embedding category is defined in the text.

Figure 2: SNR versus realized DiBA storage ratio for the 40 selected matrices in Experiment 1. Each curve corresponds to one selected matrix, and markers correspond to the swept intermediate dimensions k. Colors indicate functional categories. The horizontal axis is the realized ρDiBA(k; 16) computed from each matrix shape, not k itself.
Figure 4: Per-layer reconstruction SNR for AST attention projections.

Table 3: Speech Commands results for Experiment 2-2. Target ρ and compression are theoretical values for the 48 replaced attention-projection weight+bias components only, with the corresponding FP32 dense components counted as 1; they are not full-model compression ratios.

Figure 3: Held-out accuracy versus target-component theoretical storage ratio for Experiments 2-1 and 2-2. The horizontal axes are not full-model compression ratios; arrows denote scale-only retuning with fixed binary factors or fixed integer codes.
